lines.en_lit: ninety ris seven and a half for each mil, At each and every booth, say to him: Here is food; here is water, And they escort him from booth to booth, except for the last who does not reach the cliff with him. Rather, he stands from a distance and observes his actions

What did do He divided a strip of crimson half tied to the rock, and half of it tied between the two horns And he pushed the goat backward, and it rolls and descends. And it would not reach halfway the mountain until it was limb limb. came and sat under the last booth until it grows dark And from what point are the garments rendered impure, From he emerges outside the wall of Jerusalem. Rabbi Shimon says: from the moment that he pushes from the cliff.

GEMARA: The Sages taught: There ten booths, and twelve mil; the statement of Rabbi Meir. Rabbi Yehuda says: nine booths and ten mil. Rabbi Yosei says: five booths and ten mil, and all of them by a joining
Rabbi Yosei said: Elazar my son told me: If by an eiruv, I could even make two booths ten mil.

In accordance with whose that which was taught except for the last who did not reach the cliff with him; rather, he stands from a distance and observes his actions? In accordance with whose in accordance with Rabbi Meir,

At each and every booth say to him: Here is food; here is water, taught: No man ever needed this However, because one who has bread in his basket is not similar to one who does not have bread in his basket.
What did do He divided a strip of crimson Let him tie the whole to the rock. Since it is a mitzva the goat perhaps it would turn white quickly, and his mind would be eased
let him tie the whole between horns. Sometimes the goat turns its head and will not know

The Sages taught: At first they would tie strip of crimson to the opening of the Entrance Hall on the outside. turned white they would rejoice, it did not turn white they would be sad and ashamed. they established that they should tie to the opening of the Entrance Hall on the inside, And would still peek and see: it turned white, they would rejoice, it did not turn white they would be sad. established that they should tie half of to the rock and half of it between horns,

Rabbi Naḥum bar Pappa said in the name of Rabbi Elazar HaKappar: At first they would tie the strip of crimson to the opening of the Entrance Hall on the inside, and when the goat reached the wilderness, would turn white, and they knew that the mitzva was fulfilled. is stated: “Though your sins be as scarlet, they will become white as snow;
would not reach halfway the mountain A dilemma was raised before What those limbs? pleasure Rav and Shmuel One said they are permitted and one said they are prohibited.
The one who said they are permitted
